content.problemStatement:
An array is said to be **good** if all its elements are distinct, i.e. no two elements of the array are equal to each other.

You are given a positive integer NNN and an integer KKK such that N≤K≤(N+12)N \leq K \leq \binom{N+1}{2}N≤K≤(2N+1​).

Construct an array AAA of length NNN that satisfies the following conditions


AAA has **exactly** KKK good (contiguous) subarrays, and
Every element of AAA is an integer from 111 to NNN (both inclusive).
If there are multiple such arrays, you can print **any** of them.
constraints: The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of testcases. The description of the TTT testcases follow.
Each testcase consists of a single line with two space separated integers, NNN and KKK respectively.
For each testcase print NNN space separated integers, the elements of the constructed array.
If there are multiple outputs, you can print **any** of them.
Your output will be considered correct only if the following conditions are satisfied,
Every element of the array is between 111 and NNN, and
The array has exactly KKK good subarrays
Sum of NNN over all testcases is atmost 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
